steps.title: Identify the defining signal of relentless commitment
steps.content:
The passage opens with a controlling simile—”Louis Pipestone tended the petition like a garden”—emphasizing constant care and attention.

This is immediately reinforced: he “kept it with him at all times” and seeks out signers everywhere—at the gas pump, the mercantile, at Henry's, on the road, outside the clinic.

Across all these settings and circumstances, the passage constructs a single, focused portrait of persistent personal dedication.

steps.title: Match the signal to the correct answer
steps.content:
Choice C correctly identifies the passage's purpose: portraying Louis's strong commitment to collecting signatures through his relentless, systematic effort.

The other three misread what the passage is actually about—claiming it traces attitude shifts, judges tribal enthusiasm, or explains why people refuse—when the text focuses exclusively on Louis's personal dedication.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
